page: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached small-block reads, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidth falls into the average tier. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES, VAES, and AVX.

With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 10.67 GB per core and fast local NVMe storage, this instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. However, it exhibits poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation, making it less suitable for heavy AI inference without dedicated GPU acceleration. It offers a balanced profile for high-density memory tasks and local storage caching.
description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor. The system delivers strong multi-core CPU and memory performance, placing in the top 25% on stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks. While its cached memory read speeds are highly efficient, uncached large-block transfers and large LLM text generation tasks show average to poor performance. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB, this instance is optimized for memory-heavy applications, multi-threaded data processing, and database workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An x86_64 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz
512 GB of DDR4 memory for memory-intensive applications
1880 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory read performance
Best suited for database operations and multi-threaded data processing
